stops: Route 66 Begin Sign – E Adams & S Michigan Ave, Chicago
Gemini Giant muffler man in Wilmington (quick photo)
Chain of Rocks Bridge – old Rt66 Mississippi crossing
Gateway Arch St. Louis – tram ride $16 if you arrive early enough

sleep.spot: Cahokia Mounds State Park lot (East St. Louis side)
sleep.notes: Quiet, safe, overnight historically tolerated. Or Hollywood Casino lot, St. Louis.

groceries: Schnucks on Hampton Ave, St. Louis
warn: ⚠️ Skipping Springfield — long first day. Leave Chicago by 8am to enjoy stops along the way.

stops: Meramec Caverns near Stanton – cave tour $26
Cuba MO murals – 30 min walk on Rt 66 main street
Blue Whale of Catoosa OK – free Route 66 icon
Pops 66 Soda Ranch Arcadia – giant glowing bottle, 700 sodas

sleep.spot: Riverwind Casino or Chickasaw NRA lot, south of OKC
sleep.notes: Oklahoma casinos very car-camper friendly. Chickasaw NRA is free federal land.

warn: ⚠️ Longest driving day of the trip. Leave St. Louis by 7am. Worth it to set up the West.

stops: National Cowboy & Western Heritage Museum OKC – $15, world-class Western art & history (your kind of place)
Route 66 Museum Clinton OK – $5, best on the Mother Road
U-Drop Inn Shamrock TX – Art Deco icon, free photo
Cadillac Ranch Amarillo – FREE, 24/7, bring spray paint
dark: Good – Chickasaw NRA, away from city

sleep.spot: Palo Duro Canyon State Park campground ($20)
sleep.notes: 'Grand Canyon of Texas'. Canyon walls glow red at sunset. Wake up inside the canyon.

stops: Adrian TX Midpoint Café – exact midpoint Chicago↔LA (early stop)
Santa Fe Plaza – quick 2h stop: oldest capital in USA, adobe Old West architecture, lunch
Drive north into Utah's red rock country
Arrive Moab evening – gateway to Arches & Canyonlands
dark: ⭐ Excellent – Moab BLM, red-rock dark skies

sleep.spot: BLM dispersed along Hwy 128 (Colorado River) or Willow Springs Rd, Moab
sleep.notes: Free camping right by the red cliffs along the river. Willow Springs Rd is the classic free Moab spot.

warn: ⚠️ Longer day — Santa Fe is now a quick 2h stop, not an overnight. Leave Amarillo by 7am. Worth it for Moab.

stops: Arches NP morning – ATB covers entry: Delicate Arch (the big ring arch ⭐), Windows, Balanced Rock
Hwy 128 Colorado River Scenic Byway – the road beside the giant orange canyon walls 🧡
Drive south through Navajo Nation red-mesa country
Forrest Gump Point + Monument Valley Tribal Park ($8) – Valley Drive 17-mi loop
wildlife: Arches/Canyonlands – desert bighorn sheep, jackrabbits, ravens
Navajo Nation desert – coyotes, hawks
Wild horses occasionally roam the mesas
movies: 🎬 Arches & Moab – Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade (opening), Thelma & Louise, 127 Hours, Mission Impossible 2
🎬 Monument Valley – Stagecoach, The Searchers, Forrest Gump (the run), Back to the Future III
🎬 Forrest Gump Point on Hwy 163 – the exact spot he stops running
dark: ⭐ WORLD-CLASS – Monument Valley / Mexican Hat, almost zero light pollution

sleep.spot: Goulding's Campground ($30) or BLM near Mexican Hat, UT (FREE)
sleep.notes: Goulding's has the classic Monument Valley view. BLM near Mexican Hat is free, 20 min north.

groceries: Stocked in Moab — very limited shops in Navajo Nation
warn: ⚠️ Do Arches early (gates can require timed entry in peak season — check nps.gov). Then drive south. Delicate Arch hike is 3mi round trip, go before the heat.

stops: Monument Valley sunrise – buttes glowing orange at dawn (don't miss)
Desert View Watchtower – first Grand Canyon view from the east, most dramatic
Grand Canyon South Rim – Mather Point, Yavapai Point, rim trail walk
Hopi Point sunset – best sunset spot on the rim
wildlife: California condors soaring along the rim (rare, huge wingspan)
Elk and mule deer in the village area
Rock squirrels, ravens
movies: 🎬 Grand Canyon – Thelma & Louise (climactic ending filmed near here), National Lampoon's Vacation
dark: ⭐ Excellent – Kaibab NF, Grand Canyon dark-sky park

sleep.spot: Kaibab National Forest dispersed (just south of park entrance)
sleep.notes: Free dispersed camping right outside the park. Very popular, arrive by late afternoon.

groceries: Stock up at Tuba City or in the park village store

stops: Grand Canyon sunrise at Mather Point – arrive 30 min before
Route 66 in Seligman AZ – the town that inspired Pixar's Cars, pure nostalgia
Oatman AZ – wild burros roaming the street, ghost town (slight detour, very cowboy)
Las Vegas Strip at night – arrive after dark, top down 🎰
wildlife: Oatman AZ – wild burros walking the street (will approach for food)
Desert bighorn sheep in the Black Mountains
movies: 🎬 Seligman AZ – the real town that inspired Radiator Springs in Pixar's Cars
🎬 Kingman/Oatman Route 66 – How the West Was Won, Easy Rider vibes
dark: Fair – BLM near Jean, some Vegas glow on horizon

sleep.spot: BLM land near Jean, NV (exit 12, I-15 south of Vegas)
sleep.notes: Classic Vegas car-camper spot, 20 min south of the Strip.

stops: Seven Magic Mountains – colorful boulder art south of Vegas, free
Barstow CA – Route 66 Mother Road Museum, free
Santa Monica Pier – official END of Route 66 🏁 (you made the Mother Road!)
Venice Beach sunset
movies: 🎬 Santa Monica Pier – Forrest Gump, Iron Man, The Net
🎬 Seven Magic Mountains – music videos & car commercials

sleep.spot: Leo Carrillo State Beach campground, Malibu
sleep.notes: Sleep 50m from the Pacific. Reserve at reservecalifornia.com — fills fast in summer.

groceries: Ralphs on Lincoln Blvd, Santa Monica

stops: Griffith Observatory + Hollywood Sign view – free
Hollywood Walk of Fame + TCL Chinese Theatre
Getty Center – free entry, book timed slot at getty.edu
Beverly Hills + Rodeo Drive drive-through

sleep.spot: Leo Carrillo State Beach OR Malibu Creek SP
sleep.notes: Stay coastal — best sleep near LA, away from city heat & noise.

stops: Universal Studios OR Santa Monica/Venice beach day
Downtown LA – The Last Bookstore, Grand Central Market
Malibu beaches – El Matador State Beach (most scenic)
Sunset at El Matador or Point Dume

sleep.spot: Leo Carrillo State Beach, Malibu
sleep.notes: Last coastal night before heading up PCH.

stops: Malibu coast cruise – top down
Bixby Creek Bridge Big Sur – THE photo of PCH
McWay Falls – waterfall onto the beach, Big Sur
Elephant Seal Colony San Simeon – free, hundreds of seals
dark: Good – Big Sur coast, dark to the west over ocean

sleep.spot: Kirk Creek Campground ($25) – on a cliff above the Pacific
sleep.notes: One of the best campsites in America — bluff 100ft above the ocean. Book weeks ahead at recreation.gov.

stops: 17-Mile Drive Pebble Beach – $12.25, stunning coast on the way up
Golden Gate Bridge – walk or drive across, photo from Battery Spencer (Marin side)
Chase Center – home of the Warriors 🏀 (photo / team store)
Painted Ladies at Alamo Square + Fisherman's Wharf / Pier 39 sea lions
wildlife: Pier 39 – wild California sea lions barking on the docks (free, year-round)
Año Nuevo / coast – harbor seals
Pelicans & cormorants along the bay
movies: 🎬 Golden Gate Bridge – Vertigo, San Andreas, Ant-Man
🎬 Alcatraz – The Rock (1996)
🎬 Fisherman's Wharf area – The Princess Diaries

sleep.spot: Rob Hill Campground (Presidio) OR Pacifica State Beach lot, south of SF
sleep.notes: Rob Hill is the only campground in SF (Presidio, book ahead). Pacifica is 20 min south, ocean-side, easier for car sleeping.

groceries: Trader Joe's or Safeway, San Francisco
warn: ⚠️ SF is tough for car sleeping in the city itself — sleep just south in Pacifica or at Presidio's Rob Hill campground.

stops: Early start from SF – inland via Hwy 120
Tunnel View – first Yosemite Valley panorama, iconic
Yosemite Valley loop – Half Dome, El Capitan, Yosemite Falls base trail
Glacier Point at sunset – 360° view, Half Dome alpenglow (drive up before dark)
wildlife: Mule deer in the meadows at dawn/dusk
Black bears (keep all food in bear lockers!)
Steller's jays, marmots near Glacier Point
movies: 🎬 Tunnel View / valley – Star Trek V, Maverick
🎬 Yosemite vistas appear in countless Westerns & The Last of the Mohicans-style epics

sleep.spot: Hodgdon Meadow Campground, Yosemite ($26)
sleep.notes: ATB Pass covers entry. Reserve campground at recreation.gov. One full day is enough for the Valley highlights + Glacier Point.

groceries: Stock up in Oakdale or Groveland before entering park
warn: ⚠️ Yosemite day-use reservation required. Book at recreation.gov (7-day rolling window, 8am). Leave SF by 7am to maximize the day.

stops: Tunnel View sunrise before leaving – magic morning light
Tioga Pass Hwy 120 – highest paved road in the Sierra, alpine lakes
Mono Lake tufa towers – eerie limestone spires, free
Bodie State Historic Park – authentic gold-rush ghost town (very Old West)
wildlife: Mono Lake – millions of migratory birds (eared grebes, phalaropes) feeding on brine shrimp
Mule deer, coyotes along Hwy 395
Yellow-bellied marmots at Tioga Pass
movies: 🎬 Bodie ghost town – backdrop feel of many Westerns
🎬 Mono Lake – High Plains Drifter (Clint Eastwood) filmed nearby; Pixar/Tim Burton visuals
dark: ⭐ Excellent – Desert Creek BLM, Nevada Great Basin

sleep.spot: Desert Creek BLM, Washoe Valley south of Reno
sleep.notes: Nevada is half public land. Dark skies, free, quiet.

stops: Bonneville Salt Flats near Wendover UT – drive onto the white salt, surreal
Shoshone Falls Twin Falls – 'Niagara of the West', free
Perrine Bridge – 500ft canyon bridge walk, free
wildlife: Pronghorn antelope on the Nevada/Utah flats (fastest land animal in N. America)
Hawks & golden eagles on fence posts
Jackrabbits at dusk
movies: 🎬 Bonneville Salt Flats – The World's Fastest Indian, Independence Day, pirates/car ads

sleep.spot: Snake River Canyon rim BLM land, Twin Falls
sleep.notes: Pull off Hwy 93 south of town. Dramatic canyon views.

warn: ⚠️ Long day — break at Bonneville Salt Flats. Leave Reno by 7am.

stops: Craters of the Moon NM – ATB covers entry, lava landscape
Mormon Row barns – most photographed barns in America, Teton backdrop
Grand Teton NP – Snake River Overlook (Ansel Adams shot)
Jackson town square – elk antler arches, cowboy bars
wildlife: Grand Teton – moose at Oxbow Bend, elk, bison
Pronghorn & mule deer along the highway
Bald eagles over the Snake River
movies: 🎬 Grand Teton / Jackson Hole – Django Unchained, Spencer's Mountain, The Big Trail
🎬 Mormon Row – endless Western & photography shoots
dark: ⭐ Excellent – Bridger-Teton NF, Teton dark skies

sleep.spot: Bridger-Teton National Forest dispersed (Hwy 191 N of Jackson)
sleep.notes: Abundant pull-offs. Cool nights 45-55°F — sleeping bag essential.

groceries: Smith's or Albertsons, Idaho Falls (cheaper than Jackson)

stops: Oxbow Bend sunrise – moose & elk in the mist, Grand Teton reflection
Menor's Ferry historic district – old west homestead
Horseback trail ride in Grand Teton (book a ranch outfitter ~$70-120) — your cowboy fix
Jackson rodeo (runs Wed & Sat summer nights, ~$25)
wildlife: Oxbow Bend sunrise – moose, river otters, beavers
Elk herds in the valley
Trumpeter swans, ospreys
movies: 🎬 Grand Teton vistas – Shane (1953 classic Western), The Mountain Men

sleep.spot: Bridger-Teton NF dispersed OR Gros Ventre Campground ($35, in GTNP)
sleep.notes: Gros Ventre has stunning Teton views from camp.

stops: Old Faithful – erupts every ~90 min
Grand Prismatic Spring – Fairy Falls trail 0.5mi for the aerial view
Lamar Valley at dusk – bison herds, wolves, the 'Yellowstone series' country
Hayden Valley wildlife
wildlife: Lamar Valley – bison herds, wolves, grizzly & black bears ('America's Serengeti')
Hayden Valley – bison, elk, coyotes
Bald eagles, bighorn sheep, possible river otters
movies: 🎬 Yellowstone region – inspiration & backdrop for the 'Yellowstone' TV universe (mostly filmed in Montana/Utah, but this is the real thing)

sleep.spot: Madison Campground ($30) OR Gallatin NF near Gardiner MT (FREE)
sleep.notes: Book Madison at recreation.gov early. Gallatin NF outside north entrance is free.

groceries: Stock fully in Jackson — last real grocery store
warn: ⚠️ Book Yellowstone campground at recreation.gov ASAP — fills months ahead.

stops: Mammoth Hot Springs terraces – morning light
Yellowstone Lower Falls + Grand Canyon of Yellowstone
Drive the dramatic East Entrance (Buffalo Bill Scenic Byway)
Cody WY – founded by Buffalo Bill; Cody Nite Rodeo runs nightly in summer (~$25) 🐎
wildlife: Bighorn sheep on the East Entrance cliffs
Bison & elk in northern Yellowstone
Mule deer around Cody
movies: 🎬 Cody – Buffalo Bill country, spiritual home of the Western genre
🎬 Shoshone Canyon – rugged Western film scenery

sleep.spot: Buffalo Bill State Park or Shoshone NF dispersed, near Cody
sleep.notes: Shoshone National Forest (first NF in the USA) west of Cody is free and beautiful.

warn: ⚠️ Cody Nite Rodeo is the highlight for a cowboy fan — runs every night June–Aug. Check Sept schedule.

stops: Bighorn Mountains scenic drive (Hwy 14) – dramatic mountain pass
Devils Tower National Monument – ATB covers it, first US national monument, sacred & stunning
Badlands NP at golden hour – ATB covers entry, surreal eroded spires
Wall Drug, Wall SD – classic Americana
wildlife: Devils Tower – prairie dog town at the base (huge colony, very active)
Pronghorn across the Wyoming plains
Badlands – bighorn sheep, bison, prairie dogs
movies: 🎬 Devils Tower – Close Encounters of the Third Kind (the iconic mountain!)
🎬 Badlands – Dances with Wolves region, Armageddon, Starship Troopers
dark: ⭐ WORLD-CLASS – Badlands NF, one of darkest skies in USA

sleep.spot: Badlands NP dispersed (wilderness zone) OR Buffalo Gap National Grassland
sleep.notes: Free dispersed camping. Bison wander through. Incredible dark-sky stars.

stops: Mount Rushmore at sunrise – $15 parking, ATB covers monument, no crowds
🦬 Custer State Park Wildlife Loop Rd – 18-mile loop, best guaranteed bison herds + wild burros of the whole trip ($20/vehicle, worth it)
Corn Palace Mitchell SD – world's only, 10 min
Stop in Iowa City or Des Moines
wildlife: Mount Rushmore area – mountain goats, bighorn sheep
Custer State Park (nearby) – free-roaming bison herds
Wild burros that approach cars in Custer
movies: 🎬 Mount Rushmore – North by Northwest (Hitchcock), National Treasure 2

sleep.spot: Walmart or casino lot, Des Moines/Iowa City area
sleep.notes: Last car-camp night. Easy 4h to Chicago tomorrow.

groceries: Walmart, Mitchell or Sioux Falls SD
warn: ⚠️ Long day, but splits the return into two manageable drives instead of one brutal 12h.

stops: Easy morning drive east on I-80
Cross the Mississippi at the Quad Cities
Arrive Chicago early afternoon — return the Mustang
Rest with your uncle before Sep 13 flight to Paris ✈️

sleep.spot: Uncle's home, Chicago 🎉
sleep.notes: 22 days, ~6,400 miles. From Route 66 to Yellowstone and back. Mabrouk!
